title: How to turn LinkedIn into a 20+ calls/month acquisition channel in 90 days
author: Vasilije Simic (Undecagon)
steals: 4-step LinkedIn Inbound Funnel: Content Strategy → Profile Optimization → DM Conversion → Launch
Your LinkedIn profile is not a resume. It's your most important landing page.
Every section must answer: 'Is this person worth talking to?'
LinkedIn is still in early days as an acquisition channel — B2B competition is 'embarrassingly low'
Warm DM outreach (people who've already seen your content) converts far better than cold
DMs structured as natural conversations, not sales pitches — lead with value first
Consistent 5-7x/week posting is the minimum for inbound funnel momentum
Daily engagement routine: commenting on ICP posts, not just posting your own content
Case studies as content pillars (4 case studies in the doc)
Lead magnets: 8 custom-built per month (Nebula's free audit is the lead magnet)
Profile headline must clearly state who you help and what outcome you deliver
About section written like a sales page: Problem → Solution → Proof → CTA
Featured section reframed around relevant wins and results
Connection requests + DMs daily as part of a clean system
Tracking dashboard: profile views, connection growth, conversations started, calls booked
Avg 3 weeks to see first booked calls with proper system
$5,000-$20,000 MRR target in 90 days from LinkedIn inbound
nebula_adaptation: Created growth_system/linkedin_profile_optimization.md (section-by-section guide for Mike's profile). Added warm_dm_sequence (3-touch flow) to linkedin_reply_templates.json. Created growth_system/daily_engagement_routine.md (17min/day system). Created growth_system/content_calendar_meta.json (5-7x/week cadence with weekend post formats). Updated linkedin-post-of-the-day cron to load all new context files.
